extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR soloed to victory in final kilometer after catching QUINTANA and VALVERDE on gravel section, LÓPEZ cracked dramatically from commanding position.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA counterattacked LÓPEZ on gravel section with 3km remaining, demonstrating Movistar's willingness to attack race leader opportunistically.
VALVERDE's premature attack with 3km left proved tactically wrong, forcing teammates to chase and reunite group before POGAČAR countered.
FUGLSANG forced to wait for LÓPEZ despite being in stage-winning move, revealing team orders prioritize GC over stage glory.
BEVIN set grinding tempo early on climb, then sat up letting BIZKARRA take KOM points—legal but questionable team riding ethics.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: At 20 years old, attacked final kilometer after others weakened themselves, showing maturity to wait for optimal moment.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: Cracked spectacularly from commanding position when QUINTANA attacked on gravel, losing over minute in final 3km.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Counterattack on gravel caught LÓPEZ and FUGLSANG, extending points classification lead despite Movistar tactical confusion.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Salvaged third place after being distanced, clawing back 47 seconds to sprint for time bonus.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Mistimed attack with 3km remaining forced teammates to chase, neutralizing Movistar's advantage before POGAČAR countered.
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: Strong enough for stage win but team orders forced wait for LÓPEZ, visibly frustrated throwing arms up.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: Mechanical/possible crash required bike changes from SCHULTZ then teammate bikes too large, lost contact with GC group.
rider_signals.PERNSTEINER Hermann: positive: Rode away from LÓPEZ in final kilometers, finishing fifth ahead of fading race leader.
